subject: Statistics
title: A/B Testing in Tech
description: A quick win: understand A/B Testing in Tech.

hook.text: Google once tested 41 shades of blue for a link color. The winning shade earned $200 million extra per year.

buildup.text: A/B testing randomly shows users version A or B and measures which performs better.

discovery.text: It's a randomized controlled trial — the gold standard of causal evidence — applied to web pages.

twist.text: Running too many A/B tests simultaneously inflates false positives. Companies run thousands.

climax.text: Every button, headline, and feature you see online was probably tested. A/B testing shapes the modern internet.

punchline.text: The internet you see was chosen by hypothesis tests.

quiz.question: What is A/B testing?
options: A randomized experiment comparing two versions to see which performs better
A survey asking users which design they prefer
A test that only tech companies can use
